headline: Perlis Only State Without a Climate Action Plan
context: As of March 2026, Perlis remains the only Malaysian state without a gazetted climate action plan, despite being the most flood-vulnerable state per capita.

cards.big: Perlis is Malaysia's smallest state and most flood-prone per capita.
cards.sub: It is also the only state with no climate action plan. 72% of its landmass is in a flood risk zone.

cards.big: 12 of 13 states have gazetted climate action plans since 2021.
cards.sub: Perlis state assembly has not tabled the draft prepared by LESTARI in 2023.

cards.big: Perlis flood damage averaged RM180M annually over the last 3 years.
cards.sub: State GDP is RM6.8B. Annual flood losses equal 2.6% of GDP — the highest ratio nationally.

cards.big: Sea level rise projections show 8% of Perlis below 2m elevation.
cards.sub: Without adaptation, coastal agriculture in Kuala Perlis faces permanent saltwater intrusion by 2040.

cards.big: The state most vulnerable to climate change is the only one without a plan for it.
Small states need federal support to develop climate plans. Perlis's omission is a failure of both state initiative and federal coordination.
